mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of rate-increase months.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month rise in the unemployment rate signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by > 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of triggered instances or Rank IC remains below 0.01 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate a broad-based demand contraction, reducing corporate revenue expectations and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 58% of co-declining months or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-recession recovery phase signals expanding capacity utilization and rising corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of recovery-phase acceleration months or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth reflects stronger aggregate demand and corporate earnings momentum, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as markets price in improved fundamentals.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in > 52% of high-GDP quarters or Rank IC remains below 0.015 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price inflation above the 3.5% threshold forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, increasing real discount rates and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of high-inflation months or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) when inflation remains above threshold for > 6 months.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, boosting consumer spending expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of joint-improvement months or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts during a late-cycle expansion signals weakening consumer balance sheets and reduced construction sector activity, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 58% of housing decline months during late-cycle phases or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.
